items.briefing:
A new report from law firm Clayton Utz is urging Australian boards to take third-party supplier risk and rushed AI rollouts more seriously, warning that gaps in oversight are creating real exposure for organisations. Separately, security researchers have linked a malware campaign called "ClickFix" to fake software update prompts — if staff see an unexpected pop-up asking them to run an update or copy a command, they should ignore it and report it. INC ransomware groups are also actively targeting organisations by exploiting basic security weaknesses, such as weak passwords and unpatched systems.

Over 30,000 Fortinet network devices have been compromised globally in a large-scale credential theft campaign, which may affect Australian businesses using this equipment. If your organisation uses Fortinet products, check with your IT team that firmware is up to date and that no unusual access has occurred.

**Practical tip:** Never click "update" or run commands

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure attack on major Australian food exporter signals vulnerability across supply chain.
